prompt:
You are a blind ceiling-quality judge. You receive one task and three anonymized candidate patches. Do not infer or mention model, arm, attempt, harness, or author identity; judge only the task text and diffs.

Task text:
Add support for item_comments to syndication framework
Description
	
Add comments argument to feed.add_item() in syndication.views so that item_comments can be defined directly without having to take the detour via item_extra_kwargs .
Additionally, comments is already explicitly mentioned in the feedparser, but not implemented in the view.

P1 patch.diff:
```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..a531fcfc3a 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -199,10 +199,17 @@ class Feed:
             if updateddate and is_naive(updateddate):
                 updateddate = make_aware(updateddate, tz)
 
+            item_extra_kwargs = dict(self.item_extra_kwargs(item))
+            if 'comments' in item_extra_kwargs:
+                comments = item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')
+            else:
+                comments = self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item)
+
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
                 description=description,
+                comments=comments,
                 unique_id=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_guid', item, link),
                 unique_id_is_permalink=self._get_dynamic_attr(
                     'item_guid_is_permalink', item),
@@ -214,6 +221,6 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
-                **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
+                **item_extra_kwargs
             )
         return feed
diff --git a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
index 13aa944e8a..993f1e2ff8 100644
--- a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
+++ b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
@@ -673,6 +673,22 @@ This example illustrates all possible attributes and methods for a
             Returns the URL for every item in the feed.
             """
 
+        # ITEM COMMENTS URL -- One of the following three is optional. This is
+        # only applicable to RSS feeds.
+
+        def item_comments(self, item):
+            """
+            Takes an item, as returned by items(), and returns the item's
+            comments URL as a normal Python string.
+            """
+
+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments URL for every item in the feed.
+            """
+
+        item_comments = 'https://www.example.com/comments/' # Hard-coded comments URL.
+
         # ITEM_GUID -- The following method is optional. If not provided, the
         # item's link is used by default.
 
diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py b/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
index 4e9b1170b1..5d7bc6f553 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
@@ -49,6 +49,18 @@ class TestRss2FeedWithGuidIsPermaLinkFalse(TestRss2Feed):
         return False
 
 
+class TestRss2FeedWithComments(TestRss2Feed):
+    def item_comments(self, item):
+        return 'http://example.com/blog/%s/comments/' % item.pk
+
+
+class TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs(TestRss2Feed):
+    def item_extra_kwargs(self, item):
+        return {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/%s/comments/' % item.pk,
+        }
+
+
 class TestRss091Feed(TestRss2Feed):
     feed_type = feedgenerator.RssUserland091Feed
 
diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
index dab7ebab33..5ff7fe7688 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
@@ -176,6 +176,24 @@ class SyndicationFeedTest(FeedTestCase):
                 item.getElementsByTagName('guid')[0].attributes.get(
                     'isPermaLink').value, "true")
 
+    def test_rss2_feed_with_comments(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        items = doc.getElementsByTagName('channel')[0].getElementsByTagName('item')
+
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+
+    def test_rss2_feed_with_comments_from_extra_kwargs(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments-extra/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        items = doc.getElementsByTagName('channel')[0].getElementsByTagName('item')
+
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+
     def test_rss2_single_enclosure(self):
         response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/single-enclosure/')
         doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py b/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py
index bff7bd0c8d..23904b5bfb 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py
@@ -10,6 +10,10 @@ urlpatterns = [
     path(
         'syndication/rss2/guid_ispermalink_false/',
         feeds.TestRss2FeedWithGuidIsPermaLinkFalse()),
+    path('syndication/rss2/comments/', feeds.TestRss2FeedWithComments()),
+    path(
+        'syndication/rss2/comments-extra/',
+        feeds.TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs()),
     path('syndication/rss091/', feeds.TestRss091Feed()),
     path('syndication/no_pubdate/', feeds.TestNoPubdateFeed()),
     path('syndication/atom/', feeds.TestAtomFeed()),

```

```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..7855963652 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -199,6 +199,12 @@ class Feed:
             if updateddate and is_naive(updateddate):
                 updateddate = make_aware(updateddate, tz)
 
+            item_extra_kwargs = self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
+            if 'comments' not in item_extra_kwargs:
+                item_extra_kwargs = {
+                    **item_extra_kwargs,
+                    'comments': self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item),
+                }
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
@@ -214,6 +220,6 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
-                **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
+                **item_extra_kwargs
             )
         return feed

         item_updateddate = datetime.datetime(2005, 5, 3) # Hard-coded updateddate.
 
+        # ITEM COMMENTS URL -- It's optional to use one of these three. This is
+        # a hook that specifies how to get the comments URL for a given item.
+
+        def item_comments(self, item):
+            """
+            Takes an item, as returned by items(), and returns the item's
+            comments URL.
+            """
+
+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments URL for every item in the feed.
+            """
+
+        item_comments = 'https://www.example.com/comments' # Hard-coded comments URL.
+
         # ITEM CATEGORIES -- It's optional to use one of these three. This is
         # a hook that specifies how to get the list of categories for a given
         # item. In each case, the method/attribute should return an iterable

@@ -49,6 +49,11 @@ class TestRss2FeedWithGuidIsPermaLinkFalse(TestRss2Feed):

diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
index dab7ebab33..e47d4df2d1 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
@@ -176,6 +176,21 @@ class SyndicationFeedTest(FeedTestCase):
                 item.getElementsByTagName('guid')[0].attributes.get(
                     'isPermaLink').value, "true")
 
+    def test_rss2_feed_item_comments(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        chan = doc.getElementsByTagName(
+            'rss')[0].getElementsByTagName('channel')[0]
+        items = chan.getElementsByTagName('item')
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+        for item in items:
+            self.assertChildNodes(item, [
+                'title', 'link', 'description', 'guid', 'category', 'pubDate',
+                'comments', 'author',
+            ])
+
     def test_rss2_single_enclosure(self):
         response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/single-enclosure/')
         doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)

     path('syndication/atom/', feeds.TestAtomFeed()),
```

P3 patch.diff:
```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..a09cc5fe74 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -214,6 +214,7 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
+                comments=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item),
                 **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
             )
         return feed
diff --git a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
index 13aa944e8a..26faef59cb 100644
--- a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
+++ b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
@@ -889,6 +889,22 @@ This example illustrates all possible attributes and methods for a
 
         item_copyright = 'Copyright (c) 2007, Sally Smith' # Hard-coded copyright notice.
 
+        # ITEM COMMENTS (only applicable to RSS feeds) -- One of the following
+        # three is optional. The framework looks for them in this order.
+
+        def item_comments(self, obj):
+            """
+            Takes an item, as returned by items(), and returns the item's
+            comments as a normal Python string.
+            """
+
+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments for every item in the feed.
+            """
+
+        item_comments = 'http://example.com/comments' # Hard-coded comments.
+
```

Rank P1/P2/P3 on exactly these axes: design_coherence, robustness, spec_long_horizon_consistency, maintainability_api_ergonomics.
Use this strict JSON schema and no markdown fences:
{"axes":{"design_coherence":{"tiers":[["P1","P2","P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[]},"robustness":{"tiers":[["P1","P2","P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[]},"spec_long_horizon_consistency":{"tiers":[["P1","P2","P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[]},"maintainability_api_ergonomics":{"tiers":[["P1","P2","P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[]}}}
Ties are allowed only by putting indistinguishable packets in the same tier. For every strict winner-loser pair created by tiers, include exactly one concrete one-sentence delta citing something visible in the diffs.

strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 keeps `comments` as its own explicit named argument in the `feed.add_item()` call, matching every sibling hook, while P2 merges the computed value into `item_extra_kwargs` and only exposes it via `**item_extra_kwargs`, hiding it from the explicit argument list.

P1 explicitly pops any pre-existing `'comments'` key out of `item_extra_kwargs` (`item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')`) before unpacking it, whereas P3 appends `comments=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item)` next to the unmodified `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)`, leaving two independent paths that can both supply the same key.

P2's `if 'comments' not in item_extra_kwargs` check integrates the new hook into the existing extra-kwargs resolution mechanism, while P3 adds `comments=` as a fully separate, unguarded kwarg alongside the untouched `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)`.

P1 adds `test_rss2_feed_with_comments_from_extra_kwargs`, directly exercising the case where `item_extra_kwargs()` already defines `'comments'`, while P2's equivalent precedence logic has no corresponding test.

P1's `comments = item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')` branch prevents `feed.add_item()` from ever receiving `comments` twice, whereas P3's unconditional `comments=` plus `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)` will raise `TypeError: got multiple values for keyword argument 'comments'` if `item_extra_kwargs()` returns a `'comments'` key.

P2's dict-merge guard (`{**item_extra_kwargs, 'comments': ...}` only when absent) avoids ever passing `comments` twice to `add_item()`, while P3 has no such guard and will crash under the identical scenario.

P1's dedicated `TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs` fixture locks in the exact 'detour via item_extra_kwargs' scenario named in the ticket as a permanent regression guard, while P2 implements but never tests that scenario.

P1 satisfies both ticket sentences with tests for each — a direct `item_comments` hook and continued support for feeds using the `item_extra_kwargs` detour — while P3 only satisfies the first and silently regresses the second.
P2 preserves the pre-existing `item_extra_kwargs`-based comments mechanism the ticket explicitly references, while P3's `views.py` change has no such preservation and will break feeds relying on it.

P1's two isolated test subclasses (`TestRss2FeedWithComments`, `TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs`) each cover one behavior cleanly, while P2 covers only the new-hook case, leaving the extra_kwargs branch without direct test evidence.

P1 introduces a separately-named `TestRss2FeedWithComments` fixture, while P3 adds `item_comments` straight onto the shared base `TestRss2Feed` class using the same literal string already used for `item_copyright`, coupling the new field to every existing subclass and weakening the test's ability to detect a copyright/comments mix-up.

P2 tests its new hook via an isolated subclass with a distinct comments URL value, while P3 mutates the shared `TestRss2Feed` base fixture and reuses the copyright string as the comments value, reducing test isolation and assertion power.
